title: Swarm feeds are sequences of SOCs at predictable addresses that act as mutable pointers
content:
## Claim

Swarm **feeds** are mutable pointers built as a sequence of SOCs whose addresses are deterministically derivable from `(owner, topic, index)`.

## Evidence

- Feed address: `Keccak256(Keccak256(topic || index) || owner_address)`
- Topic is an arbitrary 32-byte identifier (the adapter uses `ph:v2:doc:<docId>`, `ph:v2:drive:<driveId>`, etc.)
- Only the owner private key can publish updates; anyone knowing `owner + topic` can read
- Typical feed payload is 8-byte timestamp + 32-byte Swarm reference
- Two indexing schemes:
  - **Sequential**: 0, 1, 2, ... — best for ordered updates and messaging
  - **Epoch-based**: nested time epochs with binary search — best for sporadic/irregular updates
- The adapter uses sequential indexing via bee-js `feedIndexNext` header auto-detection

## Why it matters

Feeds let Swarm support named, mutable resources despite chunks being immutable. They are the transport for every manifest update our adapter writes.
description: Feed address = Keccak256(Keccak256(topic || index) || owner). Sequential or epoch-based indexing; only owner writes. Used for manifests in our adapter.
